data.title: A trader spent over $700,000 to buy VVV and made a total profit of $430,000.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, trader @changefomo spent over $700,000 to buy VVV tokens, and his current holdings are worth nearly $1 million. His total profit on the FOMO platform has reached $430,000.

data.title: India's Role as a Global Capability Center Expands
data.description: India is increasingly becoming a significant hub for global capability centers, highlighting its expanding influence in the global business landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the country's growing importance in this sector. These centers, which provide a range of services from IT support to financial analysis, are pivotal in the operations of multinational companies. India's skilled workforce and cost-effective solutions make it an attractive destination for these centers. As more companies look to optimize their operations, India's role is expected to grow further, solidifying its position as a key player in the global market.

data.title: Crypto Trader 0x58bro Secures $7 Million in Profits from Short Positions
data.description: A crypto trader operating under the pseudonym 0x58bro has achieved $7 million in floating profits through strategic short positions. According to NS3.AI, the trader's most substantial earnings come from shorting Ethereum (ETH) and Enarx (ENA), with profits amounting to $3.7 million and $1.45 million, respectively. Despite these impressive gains, 0x58bro maintains a modest following of 1,300 on the X social media platform.

data.title: Kalshi's February Trading Volume Shows Significant Growth
data.description: Kalshi has recorded over 34.8 million trades as of February 14, with an average daily trading volume of approximately 2.5 million, according to PANews. In comparison, January saw about 54.5 million trades, indicating that February's trading volume has already reached around 64% of the previous month's total. If the current pace continues, Kalshi's monthly trading volume is on track to set a new record, potentially surpassing 69.6 million trades.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Greece to Attend Peace Committee Meeting as Observer in Washington
data.description: Greece has announced its participation as an observer in the upcoming Peace Committee meeting in Washington, marking the second phase of the Gaza ceasefire process. According to Jin10, this development comes as international efforts continue to stabilize the region and promote peace. The meeting aims to address ongoing tensions and explore solutions for lasting peace in Gaza. Greece's involvement highlights its commitment to supporting diplomatic initiatives and contributing to regional stability.

data.title: A whale with only 1,300 followers, 0x58bro, made a paper profit of $7 million by shorting assets such as ETH and ENA.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, trader 0x58bro (0x58b...f35) currently holds short positions with a floating profit of $7 million. His main profits come from shorting ETH (with a floating profit of $3.7 million) and ENA (with a floating profit of $1.45 million). It's worth noting that this trader only has 1,300 followers on the X platform.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, has seen a significant increase in sales by adopting a Bitcoin payment and reserve strategy.
data.description: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, announced on the X platform that it has seen a significant increase in sales due to its adoption of a BTC payment and reserve strategy. The company noted that BTC adoption has not only boosted brand awareness but also attracted a large number of new customers. Steak 'n Shake has now incorporated BTC into its balance sheet and plans to further expand the use of cryptocurrency in its business. (Coindesk)

data.title: Polygon's daily transaction fee revenue surpassed Ethereum's yesterday.
data.description: Cointelegraph reported on the X platform that, according to Token Terminal data, Polygon's daily transaction fees exceeded $300,000, surpassing Ethereum.

data.title: A whale sold $8.72 million worth of BTC and switched to buying 4,412 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale recently spent a total of 129 BTC (approximately $8.72 million) to buy 4412 ETH at an average price of $1976. Today, this whale added 43 BTC (approximately $2.91 million) to buy 1468 ETH. The ETH has now been transferred to a new address (0x713...4877).

data.title: Tesla Seeks AI Chip Design Engineers in Korea
data.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk has shared an announcement from Tesla AI regarding the company's recruitment of artificial intelligence chip design engineers in Korea. According to Jin10, Musk encouraged individuals in Korea interested in chip design, manufacturing, or AI software to consider joining Tesla.

data.title: US Dollar Rises Amid Expectations of Federal Reserve Rate Cuts
data.description: The US dollar has experienced a modest increase over the past two days, defying market expectations of approximately three Federal Reserve rate cuts this year. According to NS3.AI, options markets indicate a decrease in bearish sentiment toward the dollar, while money markets continue to anticipate around 64 basis points in rate cuts by the end of the year. Some strategists believe that the expectation of three rate cuts is excessive, considering ongoing economic growth and inflation levels that remain above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. This situation suggests the potential for a dollar rebound.

data.title: Tech Stocks Experience Decline in Pre-Market Trading
data.description: Some prominent technology stocks showed weakness in pre-market trading. According to ChainCatcher, AMD shares fell by 1.3%, Intel experienced a decline of nearly 1%, and Micron Technology saw a drop of over 1%.

data.title: Pedro Sanchez Intensifies Efforts to Regulate Digital Platforms
data.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has intensified his efforts to regulate digital platforms, marking the latest development in his ongoing campaign. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing scrutiny these platforms face under Sanchez's administration. The move is part of a broader strategy to ensure compliance with national regulations and protect consumer rights. Sanchez's administration has been vocal about the need for stricter oversight to address concerns related to data privacy and market competition. This initiative aligns with similar regulatory trends observed across Europe, where governments are seeking to balance innovation with accountability in the digital sector. The outcome of these efforts could have significant implications for the operations of major tech companies within Spain and potentially influence broader European regulatory policies.

data.title: India Strengthens Ties with Bangladesh After Landmark Election
data.description: India is taking swift action to reinforce its relationship with Bangladesh in the wake of last week's significant election. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of this diplomatic effort. The election results have prompted India to engage more closely with its neighbor, aiming to solidify bilateral ties and ensure regional stability. This move is seen as a strategic step to enhance cooperation and address mutual concerns between the two countries. The focus is on fostering economic collaboration and strengthening political alliances to benefit both nations.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iranian Foreign Minister Engages in Talks with Oman
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi is currently engaged in discussions with Omani officials. According to Jin10, Araqchi entered the negotiation venue a few minutes ago and is meeting with Oman's Foreign Minister Busaidi.

data.title: Analysis: The bear market is expected to continue until mid-2027. Closely monitor the crossover signal between the 90-day and 365-day moving averages.
data.description: Crypto analyst Axel stated in a blog post that the current bear market cycle began at the all-time high of approximately $125,000 in October 2025—a fact confirmed by the Entity-Adjusted Liveliness indicator, which peaked at 0.02676 in December 2025, exhibiting a typical lag compared to price, and is now reversing downwards. Historically, such reversals typically initiate an accumulation phase lasting 1.1 to 2.5 years. The chart clearly shows the previous two accumulation cycles: the 2020 bear market lasted 1.1 years, and the 2022–2024 bear market lasted 2.5 years. Both started in exactly the same way, with the green line reversing from its peak and entering a sustained decline, followed by a price drop. The current pattern is structurally identical. If this historical pattern repeats itself, this accumulation period will last at least until the end of 2026, with a more realistic expectation of until mid-2027. The key confirmation signal is when the 90-day moving average reverses downwards and breaks below the 365-day moving average (0.02622). Until this crossover occurs, the possibility of a medium-term reset and a resumption of the upward trend remains.
